SECRETS.name: All Plugins
SECRETS.description: Enable every detect-secrets plugin. Maximum recall across all known credential types: cloud providers, SaaS tokens, entropy strings, private keys, and keyword heuristics. Suitable as a broad baseline; tune confidence_threshold or disable noisy plugins (high_entropy_*, keyword) for production enforcement.

SECRETS.name: High-Confidence Cloud Credentials
SECRETS.description: Structured, vendor-specific tokens with the highest detection precision: AWS keys, GitHub/GitLab tokens, Slack webhooks, Stripe keys, Azure storage keys, OpenAI keys, PyPI tokens, and private keys. All patterns use regex heuristics built into detect-secrets with near-zero false-positive rates.

SECRETS.name: SaaS & Developer Tokens
SECRETS.description: Broad SaaS credential coverage for developer-facing content: Artifactory, Cloudant, Discord, IBM Cloud IAM/COS, JWT, Mailchimp, npm, SendGrid, Softlayer, Square OAuth, Telegram, and Twilio. Complements High-Confidence Cloud Credentials for full third-party secret coverage.

SECRETS.name: Entropy Scanner
SECRETS.description: High-entropy string detection for unstructured or custom secret formats: Base64 (limit 4.5) and Hex (limit 3.0) using Shannon entropy. Best used alongside structured plugin scans to catch secrets that don't match any known pattern. Expect higher false-positive rates; pair with a deny-list or manual review step.

SECRETS.name: Entropy Scanner — Strict
SECRETS.description: Tightened entropy thresholds to reduce false positives at the cost of some recall. Base64 limit raised to 5.5 and Hex to 3.5. Suitable for automated enforcement pipelines where manual review is unavailable.

SECRETS.name: Keyword Heuristics
SECRETS.description: detect-secrets KeywordDetector flags variable assignments whose names suggest secrets (e.g. password =, api_key =, secret =) regardless of the value. High false-positive rate by design — treat results as candidates for human review rather than confirmed secrets.

SECRETS.name: IP Address Exposure
SECRETS.description: Detects public IP addresses embedded in configuration, code, or documents. Useful for data governance audits where internal network topology must not leak into public repositories or documents.

SECRETS.name: Enterprise Full Sweep
SECRETS.description: All structured credential plugins plus entropy scanning at balanced thresholds. Covers the complete detect-secrets plugin catalog. Designed for periodic scheduled scans across large document corpora where false-positive review capacity exists.

PII.name: Global Coverage
PII.description: Detect all globally-recognized PII patterns: emails, phones, credit cards, crypto wallets, IP addresses, IBANs, URLs, and named entities (persons, locations, dates, NRP). Suitable as a broad baseline across any data source.

PII.name: US & North America
PII.description: US-specific government and financial identifiers: Social Security Numbers, driver licenses, passports, ITINs, and bank account numbers. Pair with Global Coverage for complete US compliance.

PII.name: European Union / GDPR
PII.description: GDPR-relevant entity types covering EU national identifiers, IBAN codes, medical licenses, and contact details. Designed for Article 5 data minimization and Article 35 DPIA workflows.

PII.name: DACH — Germany, Austria, Switzerland
PII.description: DACH-specific national identifiers for German tax IDs (DE_TAX_ID), Austrian social insurance numbers (AT_SVNR), and Swiss AHV pension numbers (CH_AHV), alongside EU-wide patterns for comprehensive DSGVO/DSG compliance.

PII.name: Spain
PII.description: Spanish national identifiers: NIF (Número de Identificación Fiscal) for citizens and NIE (Número de Identidad de Extranjero) for foreign residents. Combined with global contact patterns for full coverage.

PII.name: Italy
PII.description: Italian national identifiers: fiscal codes, passports, driver licenses, identity cards, and VAT codes. Covers compliance with Italy's Codice della Privacy and GDPR implementation.

PII.name: United Kingdom
PII.description: UK-specific PII including NHS numbers, combined with global contact and financial patterns. Covers UK GDPR and NHS data governance requirements.

PII.name: Australia
PII.description: Australian identifiers: Tax File Numbers (TFN), Medicare cards, ABN, and ACN. Supports compliance with the Privacy Act 1988 and Australian Privacy Principles.

PII.name: Singapore
PII.description: Singapore identifiers: NRIC/FIN national registration numbers and UEN (Unique Entity Numbers for businesses). Supports PDPA compliance.

PII.name: India
PII.description: Indian identifiers: PAN cards, Aadhaar biometric IDs, voter IDs, and vehicle registration numbers. Supports compliance with DPDP Act 2023 and IT Act requirements.

PII.name: Nordic — Finland & Poland
PII.description: Finnish personal identity codes (HETU) and Polish PESEL numbers, alongside contact identifiers. Covers local privacy law requirements for Scandinavian and Polish markets.

PII.name: Medical / Clinical
PII.description: Health-sector PII: medical license numbers, named persons, dates (appointments, DOB), locations (clinics, addresses), and contact details. Applies to clinical notes, patient records, and health system exports.

PII.name: Finance & Banking
PII.description: Financial PII: credit cards, IBAN codes, crypto wallet addresses, bank account numbers, and tax identifiers. Suitable for PCI-DSS scope assessments and financial audit workflows.

PII.name: High Confidence Global
PII.description: Maximum-precision scan across all supported entity types with an elevated confidence threshold. Best for automated enforcement pipelines where false positives are costly.

PII.name: Custom Recognizer — Employee ID
PII.description: Extends the built-in PII detector with a regex recognizer for internal employee IDs (format EMP-XXXXXX). Demonstrates how to detect proprietary identifiers alongside standard PII.

PII.name: Custom Recognizer — Deny List
PII.description: Deny-list recognizer for internal code names or sensitive project terms that should be treated as PII in external communications.

TOXIC.name: Text Safety
TOXIC.description: Balanced coverage for toxic and abusive text.

IMAGE_CLASSIFICATION.name: NSFW Detection
IMAGE_CLASSIFICATION.description: Detect sexually explicit or inappropriate images using Falconsai's NSFW model. Labels 'nsfw' and 'explicit' are flagged as critical; 'suggestive' as high; 'normal' as info.

IMAGE_CLASSIFICATION.name: General Image Classifier (ViT)
IMAGE_CLASSIFICATION.description: Baseline ImageNet-1k classification using google/vit-base-patch16-224. Returns the top-5 predicted labels. Useful as a starting point for label discovery before configuring a domain-specific model.

IMAGE_CLASSIFICATION.name: Custom Local Model
IMAGE_CLASSIFICATION.description: Use a fine-tuned model stored on disk. Point 'model' at the directory that contains config.json and the model weights. Useful for air-gapped deployments or proprietary classifiers.

IMAGE_CLASSIFICATION.name: GPU Inference
IMAGE_CLASSIFICATION.description: Run inference on the first CUDA GPU. Drop-in replacement for the CPU variant — only 'device' changes. Reduces latency by 5-10× for large image batches.

IMAGE_CLASSIFICATION.name: Multi-Label Sigmoid
IMAGE_CLASSIFICATION.description: Use sigmoid normalization for models trained with multi-label objectives (each label is scored independently). Results in all labels with score >= confidence_threshold being returned rather than a single winner.

YARA.name: Secrets — Cloud Credentials
YARA.description: Hardcoded cloud-provider credentials and API keys: AWS Access Key IDs, GitHub/GitLab tokens, Slack bot tokens, OpenAI API keys, Stripe live keys, PEM-encoded private keys, and JSON Web Tokens. Near-zero false-positive rate for all structured token formats.

rules.name: AWS_Access_Key
rules.description: AWS Access Key ID (AKIA prefix)

rules.name: GitHub_Token
rules.description: GitHub Personal Access Token (ghp_, gho_, ghs_, ghu_, ghr_ prefix)

rules.name: GitLab_Token
rules.description: GitLab Personal Access or Deploy Token (glpat- prefix)

rules.name: Slack_Token
rules.description: Slack API token — bot (xoxb), app (xoxa), user (xoxp), or refresh (xoxr)

rules.name: OpenAI_API_Key
rules.description: OpenAI API key (sk- prefix, 48+ chars)

rules.name: Stripe_Live_Key
rules.description: Stripe live secret key (sk_live_) or restricted key (rk_live_)

rules.name: PEM_Private_Key
rules.description: PEM-encoded private key block (RSA, EC, PKCS8, etc.)

rules.name: JWT_Token
rules.description: JSON Web Token — three base64url segments separated by dots

YARA.name: Malware — Process Injection & Offensive Tools
YARA.description: Detects Windows process-injection API sequences (requires 2 of 7 known APIs), known offensive tools by name (mimikatz, msfvenom, meterpreter, pypykatz, CobaltStrike), and registry run-key persistence patterns.

rules.name: Process_Injection_APIs
rules.description: Two or more process-injection Win32 API names — high-confidence indicator of reflective DLL injection or process hollowing

rules.name: Known_Offensive_Tools
rules.description: References to well-known post-exploitation and credential-dump tools

rules.name: Registry_Persistence
rules.description: Windows registry run-key persistence or scheduled-task creation

YARA.name: Suspicious Scripts — PowerShell & Shell
YARA.description: Detects PowerShell download-and-execute chains (Net.WebClient + IEX), base64-encoded commands (-EncodedCommand, FromBase64String), shell curl/wget piped to an interpreter, Python exec+encoding obfuscation, and JavaScript eval/fromCharCode patterns.

rules.name: PowerShell_Download_Exec
rules.description: PowerShell: download function combined with expression executor — classic cradle pattern

rules.name: PowerShell_Encoded_Command
rules.description: PowerShell base64 command encoding — used to evade command-line logging

rules.name: Shell_Curl_Pipe_Exec
rules.description: Shell: curl or wget output piped directly into an interpreter

rules.name: Python_Exec_Obfuscation
rules.description: Python obfuscation combining exec/compile with encoding primitives

rules.name: JavaScript_Eval_Obfuscation
rules.description: JavaScript obfuscation via eval and character-encoding primitives

YARA.name: Office Macro Threats
YARA.description: Malicious VBA macro patterns: auto-execution entry points (AutoOpen, Workbook_Open), shell spawning via WScript.Shell or ShellExecute combined with cmd.exe/PowerShell, and network downloads via MSXML2 or WinHttp paired with an HTTP URL.

rules.name: VBA_Auto_Execution
rules.description: VBA macro auto-execution entry points that run without user interaction

rules.name: VBA_Shell_Execution
rules.description: VBA macro creating a shell to run cmd.exe or PowerShell

rules.name: VBA_Network_Download
rules.description: VBA macro downloading content from the network via MSXML or WinHttp

YARA.name: PDF Threat Indicators
YARA.description: Suspicious PDF structure markers in extracted text: embedded JavaScript actions (/JavaScript, /JS, app.alert), auto-open and launch actions (/OpenAction, /Launch, /AA), and form-submit or data-import streams.

rules.name: PDF_Embedded_JavaScript
rules.description: PDF with embedded JavaScript action — can execute code on open

rules.name: PDF_Auto_Open_Action
rules.description: PDF /OpenAction, /Launch, or /AA stream — executes on document open

rules.name: PDF_Form_Submit_Or_Import
rules.description: PDF form-submission or external data-import action

YARA.name: Supply Chain — Miners, Reverse Shells & Exfil
YARA.description: Crypto-miner strings (xmrig, stratum+tcp, mining pool patterns), reverse-shell payloads (/dev/tcp, nc -e /bin/bash), obfuscated npm/pip install hooks (eval+Buffer.from, postinstall), and attacker-controlled exfiltration infrastructure (ngrok, webhook.site, interactsh).

rules.name: Crypto_Miner_Indicators
rules.description: Crypto-miner tool names or protocol strings

rules.name: Reverse_Shell_Payload
rules.description: Reverse shell via /dev/tcp, nc -e, socat+mkfifo

rules.name: Obfuscated_Install_Hook
rules.description: Obfuscated code evaluated inside an npm or pip install lifecycle hook

rules.name: Exfil_Infrastructure
rules.description: Attacker-controlled infrastructure commonly used for out-of-band data exfiltration

YARA.name: Network Threat Indicators
YARA.description: C2 framework artifacts (CobaltStrike, ReflectiveDll, Metasploit module paths), known RAT strings (QuasarRAT, AsyncRAT, NanoCore, njRAT, DcRAT, Remcos), and DNS-tunneling tools (dnscat, iodine, dns2tcp). Best applied to log exports and configuration dumps.

rules.name: CobaltStrike_Artifacts
rules.description: CobaltStrike beacon, ReflectiveDll loader, or Metasploit module path strings

rules.name: Known_RAT_Strings
rules.description: Well-known Remote Access Trojan artifact strings embedded in files or configs

rules.name: DNS_Tunneling_Tools
rules.description: DNS-tunneling tool names used to exfiltrate data over DNS queries

YARA.name: Credential Theft Patterns
YARA.description: Keylogger and keyboard-hook API names (GetAsyncKeyState, SetWindowsHookEx, WH_KEYBOARD), phishing HTML credential-form structure (password field + PHP action + submit button), and anonymous paste or file-transfer services used for credential exfiltration.

rules.name: Keylogger_APIs
rules.description: Windows keyboard-hook API references indicative of a keylogger

rules.name: Phishing_Credential_Form
rules.description: HTML form structure consistent with a credential-harvesting phishing page

rules.name: Credential_Exfil_Services
rules.description: Anonymous paste and file-transfer services frequently abused for credential exfiltration

YARA.name: Custom Rule — Internal Token Leak
YARA.description: Example of a custom rule alongside cloud-credential rules. Detects proprietary internal service tokens in format SVC-XXXXXXXX (8 hex chars) that should never appear in public documents or S3 exports.

rules.name: Internal_Service_Token
rules.description: Internal service token in SVC-XXXXXXXX format

rules.name: GitHub_Token
rules.description: GitHub Personal Access Token

LANGUAGE.name: Language Routing
LANGUAGE.description: Use high-confidence language detection when jurisdiction routing depends on it.

LANGUAGE.name: Language Baseline
LANGUAGE.description: Permissive language tagging for low-stakes analytics and indexing.

CODE_SECURITY.name: Code Security Baseline
CODE_SECURITY.description: Scan code snippets for insecure patterns using Bandit.

CUSTOM.description: GLINER2 support ticket extractor — entities + classification in one pass

config.name: Support Ticket Extractor
config.description: Extract order IDs, amounts, and intent from support tickets

CUSTOM.name: Support Ticket Extractor

CUSTOM.description: GLINER2 DACH regulatory entity extractor — entities only

config.name: DACH Regulatory Entity Extractor
config.description: Extract regulatory entities (GDPR articles, supervisory bodies, risk terms)

regulatory_body.description: Supervisory authority like BaFin, FINMA, FMA

CUSTOM.name: DACH Regulatory Entity Extractor

CUSTOM.description: GLINER2 contract risk classifier — classification only

config.name: DACH Contract Risk Terms
config.description: Detect legal/compliance risk language in DACH contracts

CUSTOM.name: DACH Contract Risk Terms

CUSTOM.description: REGEX internal product code detector — no ML required

config.name: Internal Product Code Detector
config.description: Detect internal SKU and project code leakage in public docs

CUSTOM.name: Internal Product Code Detector

CUSTOM.description: REGEX German invoice PII — postal codes, IBANs, dates
CUSTOM.name: German Invoice PII Ruleset

config.name: Austrian Legal Citation Detector
config.description: Detect references to Austrian laws and regulations

config.name: Sarcasm & Irony Detector
config.description: Detect sarcasm and irony in user-generated content

config.name: Vendor & Supplier Entity Extractor
config.description: Extract vendor names, contract values, and delivery terms

TEXT_CLASSIFICATION.name: Spam Detection
TEXT_CLASSIFICATION.description: Detect spam and unsolicited promotional content using a lightweight BERT model. Labels matching "spam" are flagged as high severity.

TEXT_CLASSIFICATION.name: Sentiment Analysis
TEXT_CLASSIFICATION.description: Classify text as positive or negative sentiment using DistilBERT. Negative predictions above threshold are flagged as info-level findings.

TEXT_CLASSIFICATION.name: Multi-label Topic Classification
TEXT_CLASSIFICATION.description: Classify documents into multiple topics simultaneously using sigmoid activation. Each label is scored independently.

TEXT_CLASSIFICATION.name: GPU Inference
TEXT_CLASSIFICATION.description: Run text classification on a CUDA GPU for high-throughput batch processing.

TEXT_CLASSIFICATION.name: Local Fine-tuned Model
TEXT_CLASSIFICATION.description: Use a proprietary model stored on disk. Useful for air-gapped deployments or domain-specific classifiers.

BROKEN_LINKS.name: Default Broken Links
BROKEN_LINKS.description: Check all links in the asset for reachability with default settings.

BROKEN_LINKS.name: Capped Scan
BROKEN_LINKS.description: Limit findings to the first 100 broken links to keep reports concise.

FEATURE_EXTRACTION.name: Semantic Embeddings (BGE — Balanced)
FEATURE_EXTRACTION.description: Produce L2-normalised mean-pooled sentence embeddings with BAAI/bge-base-en-v1.5 — a strong general-purpose encoder for semantic search, RAG, and clustering.

FEATURE_EXTRACTION.name: Lightweight Embeddings (MiniLM — Fast / CPU)
FEATURE_EXTRACTION.description: Fast, small embeddings via sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2. Ideal for CPU inference, deduplication, and high-throughput pipelines.

FEATURE_EXTRACTION.name: High-Quality Embeddings (BGE-Large)
FEATURE_EXTRACTION.description: Higher-fidelity embeddings with BAAI/bge-large-en-v1.5 for tasks where embedding quality matters more than throughput.

FEATURE_EXTRACTION.name: Multilingual Embeddings (E5)
FEATURE_EXTRACTION.description: Multilingual embeddings using intfloat/multilingual-e5-large. Suitable for enterprise content in multiple languages.

FEATURE_EXTRACTION.name: Long-Context Embeddings (ModernBERT — GPU)
FEATURE_EXTRACTION.description: Long-context embeddings for PDFs, contracts, and large documents using answerdotai/ModernBERT-base on GPU with CLS pooling.

FEATURE_EXTRACTION.name: Local Fine-tuned Embedding Model
FEATURE_EXTRACTION.description: Use a locally fine-tuned sentence encoder stored on disk. Mean pooling with normalisation applied.

OBJECT_DETECTION.name: General Object Detection (DETR Baseline)
OBJECT_DETECTION.description: Detect common objects in images using the classic DETR transformer model. Good balanced baseline for COCO-class objects.

OBJECT_DETECTION.name: Realtime Object Detection (RT-DETR)
OBJECT_DETECTION.description: Production-grade realtime detection with RT-DETR. Faster than classic DETR, strong accuracy, ideal for high-throughput image pipelines.

OBJECT_DETECTION.name: Lightweight Transformer Detector (YOLOS)
OBJECT_DETECTION.description: Pure ViT-based detector via hustvl/yolos-small. Lightweight and fast — a good starting point for research or CPU-only environments.

OBJECT_DETECTION.name: Security Screening — People & Weapons
OBJECT_DETECTION.description: Flag images containing persons, weapons, or knives with high severity. Useful for content moderation and physical security pipelines.

OBJECT_DETECTION.name: Document AI — Table & Form Detection (GPU)
OBJECT_DETECTION.description: Detect tables, forms, and document regions in scanned PDFs or screenshots using RT-DETR on GPU. Small boxes filtered out to reduce noise.

OBJECT_DETECTION.name: Custom Local Object Detector
OBJECT_DETECTION.description: Use a locally fine-tuned or exported object detection model stored on disk.
